channel.title: DesignDocuments on emsenn.net
channel.description: Recent content in DesignDocuments on emsenn.net

item.title: RFC and Design Document Processes
item.description: Requests for Comments (RFCs) and design documents are written proposals that describe a planned technical change, solicit feedback from stakeholders, and build consensus before implementation begins. The practice originates with the Internet Engineering Task Force (IETF), where Steve Crocker wrote the first RFC in April 1969 to document the ARPANET’s host software protocols. The IETF process established the core pattern: write down the proposed change, invite comment, iterate, and reach agreement before committing to code.
